rule: Kings need recognition from nobles, temples, cities, military elites and sometimes mage registries.
mechanic: legitimacy should be multi-source not one stat only

rule: City-states survive by debt, navies, information and playing larger powers against one another.
mechanic: small factions can survive through finance and diplomacy

rule: Dwarf passholds survive by controlling infrastructure larger kingdoms cannot replace.
Elven charter houses survive by making themselves legally and diplomatically indispensable.
mechanic: contract_network and memory should create non-military power

rule: Orc freeholds survive by food security, military bargaining and oath solidarity.
mechanic: recognition and service-yoke pressure drive Gharu politics

rule: Nomad rings survive by mobility, tribute extraction and refusal to be fixed into tax maps.
mechanic: nomad polities should not behave like static provinces

rule: Religious authorities can crown, shame, validate, excommunicate, protect mages or declare rituals illegal.
mechanic: religion modifies legitimacy, mage law and war justification
